# dotfiles

> Personal dotfiles managed by chezmoi. Bash-based CLI utilities use options.bash for option parsing and rich terminal output. Targets Ubuntu (Debian) and macOS.

## Chezmoi config

Template data from `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl`:
- `osId` — exact OS (e.g., `linux-ubuntu`, `darwin`)
- `osIdLike` — OS family (e.g., `linux-debian`, `darwin`)
- `wsl` — bool, running under WSL
- `codespaces` — bool, running in GitHub Codespaces
- `hasGui` — bool, machine has GUI

## Bootstrap: main.sh

`~/.local/libs/main.sh` auto-updates options.bash and sources core modules:

```bash
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/ansi.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args-version.sh
. ~/.local/share/libs/scripts/args-help.sh
```

Defines `echoRun` / `echoRunBold` (colored command echo + eval).
Aliases `doas` as `sudo` when available.

## CLI utilities (options.bash-based)

All source `~/.local/libs/main.sh`. Pattern:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euCo pipefail
shopt -s expand_aliases
. ~/.local/libs/main.sh
args::program "$script_name" "1.0" "Description"
args::option "-v, --version" "Show version"
args::option "-h, --help" "Show help"
args::parse "$@"
set -- "${args_cleaned[@]}"
```

### arx

Archive viewer/extractor.

Commands:
- `arx archive.tar.gz` — list contents (default)
- `arx list archive.tar.gz` / `arx l archive.tar.gz` — list contents
- `arx extract archive.tar.gz` / `arx x archive.tar.gz [dir]` — extract

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Supported formats: `.tar.br`, `.tar.bz2`, `.tar.gz`, `.tar.xz`, `.tar.zst`, `.tar`, `.tbz2`, `.tgz`, `.7z`, `.deb`, `.rar`, `.zip`, `.br`, `.bz2`, `.gz`, `.xz`, `.zst`, `.Z`

### upd

System updater. Template: `executable_upd.tmpl`.

Options:
- `-c, --clean` — run `cln` after updating
- `-y, --yes` — assume yes to all prompts
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Updates: `apt` (Debian), `snap` (Debian), `flatpak`, `softwareupdate` (macOS), `mas` (macOS), `brew`, `tmux` plugins, `bun`.

### cln

System cleanup. Template: `executable_cln.tmpl`.

Options:
- `-y, --yes` — assume yes to all prompts
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Cleans: `apt autoremove/autoclean` (Debian), `flatpak uninstall --unused`, `brew autoremove/cleanup`, `docker system prune`, node version management.

### jot

Encrypted S3 notes editor.

Commands:
- `jot` — list files (default)
- `jot file.md.gz.age` / `jot edit file` / `jot e file` — edit file
- `jot list [prefix]` / `jot l [prefix]` — list files
- `jot get file` / `jot g file` — download to current directory
- `jot put local_file remote_name` / `jot p ...` — upload file
- `jot delete file` / `jot del file` / `jot rm file` / `jot d file` — delete
- `jot rename old new` / `jot mv old new` / `jot r old new` — rename

Options:
- `-x, --exact` — exact file name match (not prefix)
- `-y, --yes` — create new file without confirmation
- `-n, --no` — don't create new file
- `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Environment variables:
- `JOT_AWS_S3_BUCKET` — AWS bucket name (required)
- `JOT_AGE_KEY` — age key path (default: `~/.ssh/age.key`)
- `JOT_SSH_PUB` — SSH public key (default: `~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub`)
- `JOT_SSH_PRV` — SSH private key (default: `~/.ssh/id_rsa`)

Supported codecs: `.age`, `.br`, `.bz2`, `.gz`, `.xz`, `.zst`

### goup

Run a command in the current directory and each parent directory up to `/`.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Usage: `goup "make test"` — runs `make test` in `.`, `..`, `../..`, etc.

### ollama-sync

Update all installed ollama models.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

### mount-raid

Mount a remote NFS filesystem. Template: `executable_mount-raid.tmpl`.

Options: `-v, --version`, `-h, --help`

Usage: `mount-raid server-name`

## CLI utilities (standalone)

### gpurr

Git pull with submodule recursion + pull main branch in each submodule.

### gpwiki

Git pull + pull main in `wiki/` submodule if present.

### update-dependencies

Clone or update `options.bash` sparse worktree at `~/.local/share/libs/scripts`.

### git-br

Pretty-formatted `git branch` with date, author, subject.

### git-brr

Remote branches sorted by committer date (`git-br --remote --sort=-committerdate`).

### git-bs

Branch list sorted by author date in columnar format.

### git-mbs

Filter `git-bs --all` to show only branches by the current git user.

### git-pull-main

Checkout and pull the default branch (auto-detected from `origin`).

### git-super-clean

`git reset --hard` + `git clean --force -d -x`.

## Shell aliases (dot_bash_aliases)

Key aliases and functions:

### File listing (eza)
- `l` → `eza`
- `ls` → `eza --grid --color auto --icons --sort=type`
- `ll` → `eza --long ...`
- `la` → `eza --grid --all ...`
- `lla` → `eza --long --all ...`
- `ltr` → `eza -l -t modified -r`
- `l.` → list dotfiles only

### Navigation (zoxide)
- `..` → `z ..`
- `...` → `z ../../`
- `up N` → go up N directories
- `zl [dir]` → `z dir && l`
- `mkz dir` → `mkdir dir && z dir`

### File operations
- `rm` → `rm -I --preserve-root`
- `mv` → `mv -iv`
- `cp` → `cp -iv`
- `cpg` / `mvg` → rsync-based copy/move with progress
- `rcp` / `rmv` / `rup` / `rsy` → rsync variants

### Git
- `gst` → `git status`
- `gco` → `git checkout`
- `gcob` → `git checkout -b`
- `gcm` → `git commit`
- `gbr` → `git branch`
- `grs` → `git restore`
- `gpull` → `git pull`
- `gpush` → `git push`
- `gsw` → `git switch` (default branch if no args)
- `gme` → `git merge` (default branch if no args)
- `gls` / `gll` → `git ls` / `git ll`

### Better replacements
- `cat` → `bat` / `batcat`
- `ping` → `sudo prettyping`
- `top` → `sudo htop`
- `du` → `ncdu`
- `help` → `tldr`

### SSH helpers
- `ssht host [session]` → SSH + tmux attach/new
- `mosht host [session]` → mosh + tmux
- `ett host [session]` → Eternal Terminal + tmux
- `kssh` → `kitten ssh`
- `kssht host [session]` → kitten ssh + tmux

### Other
- `where "pattern" [path/wildcard]` — recursive grep in files
- `upfind` / `upfd` — search up directory tree
- `upsearch` → `upfd -gH` (no glob expansion)
- `tre` → `tree` excluding common junk dirs
- `gre` → `grep -r` excluding common junk dirs
- `oports` → list open ports
- `psr` / `psm` / `psg` / `pst` — process listing variants
- `nowrap [col] [-col]` — truncate output to terminal width

## Environment

Set in `dot_bashrc`:
- `EDITOR=micro`
- `VISUAL=micro`
- `GITHUB_USERNAME=uhop`
- `JOT_AWS_S3_BUCKET=${GITHUB_USERNAME}-notes`
- `FORCE_COLOR=3`

## Links

- Chezmoi: https://www.chezmoi.io/
- options.bash: https://github.com/uhop/options.bash
- options.bash wiki: https://github.com/uhop/options.bash/wiki
